chapter_title.en: Chapter on What Necessitates Discretionary Punishment, Legal Punishment, Stoning, Execution, and Exile in Adultery

text: : "لاَ يُجَرَّدُ فِي حَدٍّ وَ لاَ يُشْبَحُ يَعْنِي يُمَدُّ" وَ قَالَ "يُضْرَبُ اَلزَّانِي عَلَى اَلْحَالِ اَلَّتِي يُوجَدُ عَلَيْهَا، إِنْ وُجِدَ عُرْيَاناً ضُرِبَ عُرْيَاناً وَ إِنْ وُجِدَ وَ عَلَيْهِ ثِيَابُهُ ضُرِبَ وَ عَلَيْهِ ثِيَابُهُ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5013 - Talhah ibn Zayd narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), that he said:
"A person should not be stripped of their clothing nor stretched out (meaning tied or extended) for any legal punishment."
Imam (as) also said: "The adulterer should be beaten in the state in which they are found; if they are found unclothed, they are beaten while unclothed, and if they are found clothed, they are beaten while clothed."
